themeWords.meaning: nation, people; often used of non-Israelite peoples (gentiles/heathen)
Yahweh has judged; name of a king and the symbolic valley of judgment

sentenceTheme: The LORD gathers the nations to the valley of Jehoshaphat for judgment, shakes the heavens and earth, defends Zion, and promises enduring blessing for Judah while laying waste to hostile nations.

verses.text: For, behold, in those days, and in that time, when I shall bring again the captivity of Judah and Jerusalem,

highlightedWords.meaning: Judah; the southern kingdom/tribe of Israel
Jerusalem; the chief city and capital of Israel

verses.text: I will also gather all nations, and will bring them down into the valley of Jehoshaphat, and will plead with them there for my people and for my heritage Israel, whom they have scattered among the nations, and parted my land.

highlightedWords.meaning: nation, people; gentiles/heathen
Yahweh has judged; the valley associated with divine judgment

verses.text: And they have cast lots for my people; and have given a boy for an harlot, and sold a girl for wine, that they might drink.

highlightedWords.meaning: lot, allotment; a method of decision or distribution
wine; fermented drink from grapes

verses.text: Yea, and what have ye to do with me, O Tyre, and Zidon, and all the coasts of Palestine? will ye render me a recompence? and if ye recompense me, swiftly and speedily will I return your recompence upon your own head;

highlightedWords.meaning: Tyre; a major Phoenician port city
Sidon; a principal Phoenician city north of Tyre

verses.text: Because ye have taken my silver and my gold, and have carried into your temples my goodly pleasant things:

highlightedWords.meaning: silver; money, precious metal
gold; precious metal of high value

verses.text: The children also of Judah and the children of Jerusalem have ye sold unto the Grecians, that ye might remove them far from their border.

highlightedWords.meaning: Judah; the southern kingdom/tribe of Israel
Javan; Greeks, people of the isles

verses.text: Behold, I will raise them out of the place whither ye have sold them, and will return your recompence upon your own head:

highlightedWords.meaning: to sell, give over
head; chief, top, summit

verses.text: And I will sell your sons and your daughters into the hand of the children of Judah, and they shall sell them to the Sabeans, to a people far off: for the LORD hath spoken it.

highlightedWords.meaning: son, child, descendant
daughter, girl, female descendant

verses.text: Proclaim ye this among the Gentiles; Prepare war, wake up the mighty men, let all the men of war draw near; let them come up:

highlightedWords.meaning: nation, people; gentiles/heathen
battle, war, warfare

verses.text: Beat your plowshares into swords, and your pruninghooks into spears: let the weak say, I am strong.

highlightedWords.meaning: sword; dagger or knife
spear; lance

verses.text: Assemble yourselves, and come, all ye heathen, and gather yourselves together round about: thither cause thy mighty ones to come down, O LORD.

highlightedWords.meaning: nation, people; gentiles/heathen
the personal name of Israel’s God (Yahweh)

verses.text: Let the heathen be wakened, and come up to the valley of Jehoshaphat: for there will I sit to judge all the heathen round about.

highlightedWords.meaning: valley, ravine
to judge, govern, execute judgment

verses.text: Put ye in the sickle, for the harvest is ripe: come, get you down; for the press is full, the fats overflow; for their wickedness is great.

highlightedWords.meaning: sickle; reaping hook
harvest; reaping time

verses.text: Multitudes, multitudes in the valley of decision: for the day of the LORD is near in the valley of decision.

highlightedWords.meaning: multitude, crowd, sound of a multitude
decision, determination; also a sharp threshing instrument

verses.text: The sun and the moon shall be darkened, and the stars shall withdraw their shining.

highlightedWords.meaning: sun
moon

verses.text: The LORD also shall roar out of Zion, and utter his voice from Jerusalem; and the heavens and the earth shall shake: but the LORD will be the hope of his people, and the strength of the children of Israel.

highlightedWords.meaning: the personal name of Israel’s God (Yahweh)
Zion; hill in Jerusalem, poetic name for the city/people

verses.text: So shall ye know that I am the LORD your God dwelling in Zion, my holy mountain: then shall Jerusalem be holy, and there shall no strangers pass through her any more.

highlightedWords.meaning: Zion; hill in Jerusalem, poetic name for the city/people
Jerusalem; the chief city and capital of Israel

verses.text: And it shall come to pass in that day, that the mountains shall drop down new wine, and the hills shall flow with milk, and all the rivers of Judah shall flow with waters, and a fountain shall come forth of the house of the LORD, and shall water the valley of Shittim.

highlightedWords.meaning: spring, fountain; source of water
the personal name of Israel’s God (Yahweh)

verses.text: Egypt shall be a desolation, and Edom shall be a desolate wilderness, for the violence against the children of Judah, because they have shed innocent blood in their land.

highlightedWords.meaning: Egypt; the land of the Nile
Edom; descendants of Esau and their land

verses.text: But Judah shall dwell for ever, and Jerusalem from generation to generation.

highlightedWords.meaning: Judah; the southern kingdom/tribe of Israel
Jerusalem; the chief city and capital of Israel

verses.text: For I will cleanse their blood that I have not cleansed: for the LORD dwelleth in Zion.

highlightedWords.meaning: blood; life-blood, bloodshed
Zion; hill in Jerusalem, poetic name for the city/people
